intro: Centaurea salina, also known as the saltwort or saltwort thistle, is a Mediterranean shrub belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, thanks to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Centaurea salina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Centaurea salina is often used to make herbal infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, as well as being a popular ingredient in skincare products due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
